title: Indeed Job Search App Reviews: iOS & Android User Data
description: Indeed Job Search review snapshot 2026-07-12 - 240 reviews, average 2.38 of 5, 55% 1-star, tallied per store.
answer_first: Indeed Job Search averages 2.38 of 5 across the 240 reviews in this July 12, 2026 window (App Store 2.00, Google Play 4.25); 25% are 5-star and 55% are 1-star. The first capture for this dataset was 2026-07-12 and holds 240 reviews as the comparison baseline. See the per-store, per-star breakdown in the table that follows.
facts: Across 240 reviews fetched 2026-07-12, the mean score is 2.38 of 5 (App Store 2.00, Google Play 4.25).
The 5-star share stands at 25% - 59 reviews of 240.
The 1-star share is 55%: 133 reviews out of 240.
The window draws on 1 country feed(s), led by US at 240 reviews.
updates is the leading complaint bucket: 17 of 150 low-rating reviews mention it.
Initial capture (2026-07-12) logs 240 reviews as the comparison baseline.

faq.q: Why is this average different from the store listing?
faq.a: Because it is computed only from the 240 most recent reviews captured 2026-07-12. Recent-window averages like this 2.38 of 5 move faster than lifetime store ratings and highlight what users say right now.

faq.q: What is the source of this rating breakdown?
faq.a: A single 2026-07-12 scrape of public store review feeds for Indeed Job Search: 240 reviews across 2 feed(s), each carrying its own star rating and date. The table simply counts them per star and store.

faq.q: How do the two stores compare?
faq.a: For the 2026-07-12 window: App Store 2.00, Google Play 4.25. Both figures come from the same 240-review capture, so they are directly comparable snapshots of current sentiment.

faq.q: How would I build this table for a different app?
faq.a: Run the source actor with your app's store identifiers; it emits the same normalized review records used here (240 in this window), ready to tally per star and store.

related_product.name: App Store & Google Play Review Scraper
